def: s.m. The skull, the cranium, the skull-bone; the head; the forehead; the glene; (met.) fate, destiny; — the fragment of a vessel, a potsherd; either half of a water-jar; the shell (of an egg, &c.); — a kind of leprosy
more: n. m. 1. Skull; cranium. Mār khauṅsṛon sāre kā kapāl phoṛ dūṅgā! kaisā bigāṛā hāṅḍe saë ? Rus. Ho, sister's pimp! I'll brain you with tattered shoes and smash your head! at whose misguidance are you wandering about? 2. Head; forehead. 3. Fate ( bhāg , 3). 1. Jāo Naipāl, sāth jāë kapāl . E. Prov. To Nepal who go, their fate goes with them too. 2. Jarat bilokeu jabahīṅ kapālā, Bidh ke likhe aṅk nij bhālā, Nar ke kar āpan badh bāṅchī, Haṅseū jānī Bidh girā asāṅchī . Rāmāyan. His skull in flames, upon his forehead Brahm's lines he descried, 'Die by the hand of man?' He laughed as though great Brahm had lied. 3. Ankar saṅdūr dekh āpan kapār phoṛe . Prov. On other's foreheads sendūr seen, her own head cracks with bootless spleen. kapāl-kiryā , n. f. A Hin. funeral ceremony. ety: old H. करि; Prk. करे or करिआ; S. कृत्वा, rt. कृ
def: part. (of karnā), Having done, &c.; — adv. & postpn. As, like, after the manner of; by the name or title of; by reason of, in consequence of, on account of; in respect of, as regards, as to; by means of, by, with, through (the conj. part. kar prefixed to certain verbs serves to form a class of intensives, or completives, &c.; see s.v. karnā)
subs: or kar-ke, or kar-kar-kar, or kar-kar-ke (by redupl. of kar; see ke), part., adv. & postpn. = kar (kar-ke or kar-kar is the more common form. Joined to substantives and certain adjectives it forms adverbs, e.g. kram-karke, 'respectively,' 'in order' ( = kram-se), 'gradually'; — dhruva-karke, 'firmly;' — mukhya-karke, 'principally.' Sometimes it is annexed to adverbs to emphasize them, e.g. avaśya-kar or kar-ke, 'necessarily, &c.' It is sometimes used as synonymous with se, e.g. ādhīntāʼī-karke = adhīntāʼī-se; — jal-karke snān, 'bathing with water'; — jis-karke ( = jis-se), 'by whom'; — us-karke ( = us-se), 'by him'; (see also kram-karke, above). Phrases in which kar-ke occurs are numerous, e.g. ek-ek-karke, 'one after another,' 'one at a time'; — būṅd-būṅd-karke, 'drop by drop,' 'a drop at a time'; — bahut-karke, 'in great measure,' 'at the most'; — viśesh-karke, 'particularly,' 'especially'; — satya-karke, 'in fact,' 'verily').

adj. Hard; dry; rigid; harsh, austere, stern, unfeeling, &c.
s.f. Hardness; dryness, rigidity; austerity, &c.

ety: karak˚, prob. = S. कड+कृ; cf. kaṛaknā
def: v.n. To pain, ache; to rankle (in the breast, cf. khaṭaknā); to break, snap.
more: v. n. 1. To pain; ache. 2. To rankle in the breast. 3. To break in pieces; snap.

v. n. 1. To clatter. kyā kahūṅ rāt kī bāt, sāth maiṅ pītam ke soī? Karvaṭli motī kuṛkā, motī kī gūṅj khoī. Double entendre . About last night what can I say? while with my love I lay, Turned on my side crack went my pearl, the knot just cut away. 2. To crunch; munch; gulp down. Is bahuaṛ ne dekh-kar kyoṅ na uṭṭhe āg, Chane dīye the pīsne, kuṛak gaī nir-bhāg . To see this daughter-in-law it puts me all on fire, Some pulse given her to grind she swallowed up entire. kuṛaknā , adj. Crackling; brittle. kuṛaknā kāgaz hai .

v. n. 1. To crack; crackle; crash. Jab bharatī dharatī kaṭī kaṛak gaī Sarkaṭ sārī sārī sarak gaī . Song. Ṭhumrī . Crack my waist-knot went as I was drawing water from the well, Straightway all my robe from off me sliding on the pavement fell. 2. To be beaten (drums). kaṛaknā woh naubat kā bājoṅ ke sāth, Garajnā woh dhaunsoṅ kā ḍankoṅ ke sāth . Mīr Hasan. 3. To thunder; thunder with a loud clap. Ḍar lāgat gorī saham gaī rī, Bairī bādal kaṛak rahe re, Merī piyārī ko ḍolka bhīje re . Farrukh. Wom. song. The fair one frighted pallid grew, The envious clouds then thundered too, And drenched my darling's litter through. 4. To rage; roar. 5. ( Slang ) To be off; to run away. kaṛak-kar bolnā , v. n. To speak in a loud tone, or in rage.

ety: kaṛak˚ = Prk. कड्डक्के(इ) or कड्डक्क(इ); S. कर्द+कृ
def: v.n. To crackle, crack, crash, claug; to roar, growl; to thunder; to burst into a rage; (colloq.) to make off with all speed
more: v. n. 1. To crack, to crackle. 2. To thunder.
subs: v.n. To speak in a loud tone, to burst into a rage and speak.

ety: inf. n. of کرم 'to be generous,' &c.
def: s.m. Generosity, liberality; nobleness, excellence; goodness, kindness, benignity; beneficence; bounty; grace, favour, clemency, courtesy, graciousness.
more: n. m. Graciousness; benignity; bounty (احسان and سخاوت).

n. m. 1. An act; action; work. 2. Duty; obligation. 3. Religious observances; any act of piety or religion, as sacrifices, marriage ceremonies, funeral obsequies, etc. 4. ( Gram .) The accusative case. 5. Fate ( bhāg , 3). 1. Man kare pahirau chautār, karam likhe bheṛī ke bāl . Prov. The heart on silks and satins set, sheep's wool alone her fate to get. 2. Rājā kī beṭī, aur karam kī heṭī . Prov. A princess born, her fate forlorn. 3. Man sūr, karam gaṅṛiyā . Prov. Brave heart, a coward fate. 4. Uttar jāo kī dakkhan, vohī karam ke lakkhan . Prov. Go North, go South, your fate will follow you. 5. Janam ke sāthī hain, karam ke sāthī nahīṅ . At your birth all help at hand, but fate who can withstand? 6. Janam-patr sab dekheṅ, karam-patr koī nahīṅ dekhtā . Prov. The registry of birth all see, the book of Fate none see. karmāgat , S. adj. 1. Descended or inherited in regular succession. 2. Trad…
s. m. Liberality, kindness, graciousness, benignity, clemency, bounty.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian کَرَم (karam), from Arabic كَرَم.

n. f. 1. See karāmat , 2. 1. Jā, bachchā, jā! faqīr kī karāmāt kyā ṭaṭole hai? Beggars. Go to, my son, cease power of devotee to test! ( Fac .) The penis ; yard. Langoṭe ko āge baṛhā lījīye, karāmāt, dātā, chhupā lījīye! Shauq. Your loins-strip spread out wide, your wondrous wand, friend, hide! 3. A form of address.
s.f. pl. (of kurāmat), Miracles, marvellous works or doings; miraculous powers.
more: n. f. 1. Nobleness (بزرگي 1). 2. (pl. karāmāt ) A miracle performed by a saint. 3. Supernatural power.
s. f. 1. Generosity, magnificence, nobleness, excellence. 2. A miracle. (Plur. کرامات karāmāt ).

def: s.m. Cause, motive, purpose, reason; principle, ground, source, basis; occasion; account, sake, behalf; — action; agency; — a father; — adv. or postpn. For the reason, because (of, -ke), on account (of), for the sake (of), &c.
more: n. m. S. कारण from कृ to do. 1. Cause (باعث 1, 3). 2. Sake; behalf ( khātir , 5). 1. Bhūk kaṭāve sīs ne, bhūk de jān bikāë, Baldā kāran bhūk ke kāṅdhe jūā uṭhāë . Rus. For stomachs heads cut off, for stomachs lives are given, For the stomach's sake the yoke is borne and oxen driven. 2. Sāsaṛ kāran baid bulāyā, Sauk kahe terā dhagṛā āyā . Rus. For my mother-in-law the doctor I called in, My co-wife says 'your lover is within.'
s. m. Cause, motive, reason, occasion, account.
subs: s.m. The cause of all things; an epithet of the Great Creator.
ex: یِہ سَب آپ کے کارَن ہُوَا۔

ety: inf. n. of قرّ 'to dwell, to abide'; 'to rest,' &c.
def: s.m. Dwelling, residence; fixing (one's) abode (in), settling; resting; fixedness, fixity; permanence; consistency; stability, firmness, constancy; tenacity (of purpose); — rest, repose, quietness, quiet, peace, tranquillity; quietude, patient waiting, patience; — settlement, determination, confirmation; conclusion; ratification; agreement, engagement; — reality, certainty, truth
more: n. m. قر to rest. See ستهرتائي and iqrār , 1, 3. 1. Quietude; patience. 2. Fixedness; permanence. 3. Consistency; tenacity of purpose. qarār ānā , v. n. To be at rest, or at ease. qarār pānā , v. n. To be settled ( ṭhairnā , 7, 8). qarār pāyā . 1. Arose; based. 2. Decided; determined. 3. Provided; ruled; established; prescribed; laid down. qarār-dād . 1. Establishment; settlement. 2. Engagement; agreement; contract. qarār-dād-bāham . A private bargain. qarārdād-i-jurm , The formal charge of a crime read out to a prisoner on his trial. qarār dīyā gayā . Laid down; determined. qarār denā , v. a. 1. To determine (the issues in a case) ( ṭhānnā , 2). 2. To adjudge ( tajvīz k . 1, 2). qarār k., qarār dād k. , v. a. See iqrār k . 4. 1. To take one's stand ( thānnā ). 2. To make terms; conclude (an agreement.) qarār-vāqaī , adv. 1. Definitively; positively. 2. Effectively; effectually. qarār o …

(from قرّ Heb. To rest, to cool) s. m. 1. Rest, residence, tranquillity, quietness. 2. Firmness, stability. 3. Ratification, agreement, engagement. 4. Name of the place in عراق iﻌrāḳ, where Ḥusain son of Aﻌlī was killed and buried; hence, the word is sometimes applied in India to the shrines or burying places of the Muhammadans.

To lose heart, to be anxious or distracted
The heart to come to the mouth; to be exhausted with excessive vomiting
(-ke), To be wounded in the heart; to suffer a heavy shock or blow
To suffer jealousy or envy; to repine
(-kā, or merā, &c.), To be deeply wounded, to be cut to the quick

To satisfy the heart's longing, to obtain (one's) wish; to get ease

Liver-complaint; heart-disease: kalejā muṅh-ko ānā = kalejā ulaṭnā; — (also) to feel sick (at the sight of), to loathe, abhor
